titleStmt.title: Letter from Edward O'Farrell to Judge Johnston, 27 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward O'Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Edward O'Farrell (1857-1926) the assistant under-secretary to the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland Sir Ivor Churchill Guest, Lord Wimborne. The lord lieutenant or viceroy was the monarch’s representative in Ireland. O'Farrell is writing to Judge Johnston on behalf of the lord lieutenant to send him some files relating to an application of Miss Rooney for permission to sell alcohol from her shop. O'Farrell is asking for Johnston's opinion in the matter.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/8909/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
8909 Dublin Castle 27th May, 1916. Sir, I am directed by the Lord Lieutenant to transmit to you
the accompanying file of papers relative to the application of
Miss Kate Rooney, Main St. Castleblaney,
for permission to sell Excisable Liquors until the next Quarter Sessions,
and to request that you will favour me with your opinion thereon for His
Excellency's information. I am also to request that the file of papers may be returned. I have the honour to be
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, E O'Farrell His Honour
Judge Johnston,
61 Lansdowne Road,
Dublin.
